 Artemisia tridiphylla is a plant species commonly known as 'Artemisia' or 'Sagebrush'. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Artemisia tridiphylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
